titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Victoria Mary Tyrrell 4, October 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to his sister.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
4 October 1917 My dear Minnie No news except that we are
in new billets in a large town with
lots of splendid shops restaurants etc
and we are very comfortable indeed. Have you heard of Bernie lately?
How is he getting along I have been
thinking of him quite often today
for a certain reason. Can you guess
what it is? A curious coincidence
in names - The weather is not quite so good
as it was but still it is not
raining which is a great thing.
No letters from you today yet
on account of our move. I wrote Bobby a note yesterday
just to keep him going. No news but I will write soon
again when there is any news.
with very best love to all
from
Your most affec brother
Willie Hope Daddy goes to look for
pheasants. He must and if he
asks Dr Waters to go too he
will get lifts in the motor. When
the weather gets cooler I am
expecting pheasants in parcels.
I saw 18 lovely partridge on the
road today. If only I had a
gun we should have had
partridge for dinner tonight in
spite of French Laws ___________________
